paper_title: Measurement of Luminescent Banding in Speleothems

methods.description: Selection and preparation of speleothem samples from various caves to evaluate measurement techniques based on their textures and banding patterns.

steps.description: A selection of speleothems from various caves and climatic regimes were used as test specimens.
steps.searchable_summary: select speleothem samples from diverse caves for testing

steps.description: Speleothems are identified by sample numbers that refer to a reference collection of cave material.
steps.searchable_summary: assign sample numbers to speleothems for identification

methods.description: Use of a microfocus Raman spectrometer to measure luminescence in speleothems, involving laser excitation and detection of emitted radiation.

steps.description: The base instrument was an Instruments SA Ramanor U-1000 microfocus Raman spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: set up microfocus Raman spectrometer for luminescence measurement

steps.description: The excitation source was an argon ion laser which provided blue (488 nm) or green (514.5 nm) beams.
steps.searchable_summary: configure argon ion laser for excitation

steps.description: The laser beam is guided through some steering optics into a beam splitter within the microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: guide laser beam through optics to beam splitter

steps.description: The laser beam passes through the microscope objective and is brought to a focus on the sample.
steps.searchable_summary: focus laser beam on speleothem sample

steps.description: Radiation emitted by the sample is collected by the objective lens and transmitted back to the beam splitter.
The signal is detected by a cooled photomultiplier and passes through interface electronics to the computer.
steps.searchable_summary: detect and process signal from emitted radiation

steps.description: The computer software allows data storage, manipulation, and display.

methods.description: Procedure for measuring luminescence banding across speleothem samples using a translation stage, ensuring reproducibility and accuracy.

steps.description: Samples were placed on the translation stage with the growth bands oriented perpendicular to the translation direction.
steps.searchable_summary: orient speleothem samples on translation stage

steps.description: The samples were then leveled across the entire translation distance to maintain maximum accuracy in luminescence measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: level samples for accurate luminescence measurement

steps.description: The focus was checked at both ends of the traverse and at several points in the middle.
steps.searchable_summary: check focus at multiple points along traverse

steps.description: The reproducibility of individual traverses were measured by doing one traverse, then backing off the stage and doing the same traverse a second time.
A point-by-point comparison was made and the goodness of fit, R 2 , was calculated for the entire sequence of points.
